nameMeanings.name: Taaffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Declan, Padraic.

Irish (of Welsh origin; chiefly Louth): topographic name from the River Taff (Glamorgan) or the River Taf (Pembrokeshire). The river names are Celtic or pre-Celtic in origin, and may mean ‘dark’ or ‘stream’. It may also derive from a pet form of the Welsh personal name Dafydd, the Welsh form of David , although this derivation is highly improbable.
History: An Irish family of this name held the earldom of Carlingford (Louth) in the 17th century, which they were granted as a reward for their support of the House of Stuart.

variants.name: Taffe
meanings: Irish and Welsh: variant of   Taaffe  .
German: probably from a pet nursery form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element dag-, tag- ‘day’.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Taff
meanings: Welsh and Irish: variant of   Taaffe  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

en.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle was born about 1350, in Liscartan, County Meath, Ireland. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle. He died in Liscarton Castle, County Meath, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle est né environ 1350, à Liscartan, County Meath, Irlande. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle. Il est décédé à Liscarton Castle, County Meath, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle wurde um 1350 in Liscartan, County Meath, Irland geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in Liscarton Castle, County Meath, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle è nato il circa 1350 nel Liscartan, County Meath, Irlanda. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle. Morì nel Liscarton Castle, County Meath, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castleは約1350年にアイルランドコウントイ メアスリスカータンで生まれた。彼はMrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castleとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼はアイルランドコウントイ メアスリスカートン カストルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle(은)는 대략 1350년 아일랜드 카운티 미스 리스카탠에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 아일랜드 카운티 미스 리스카턴 카스틀에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle nasceu aproximadamente 1350, em Liscartan, County Meath, Irlanda. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle. Ele faleceu em Liscarton Castle, County Meath, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle родился около 1350 г. в местности лискартань, коунти мит, Ирландия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle. Он умер в местности лискартон кастл, коунти мит, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle nació aproximadamente 1350, en Liscartan, County Meath, Irlanda. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Mrs. Sir Nicholas Taaffe of Liscarton Castle. Murió en Liscarton Castle, County Meath, Irlanda.
